strands.title: JSONL Schema Discovered — Dual-Stream Format (Request/Response Pairs)
strands.content: CYCLE 40 BREAKTHROUGH: Successfully peeked at /home/tony/projects/pichay/experiments/baseline_run1/logs/proxy_20260302_024551.jsonl (first 5 lines). Format confirmed: alternating request/response log entries, one JSON object per line. Lines 1,3,5 are requests (type='request'); lines 2,4 are responses (type='response_stream'). CRITICAL FIELDS IDENTIFIED: (REQUEST) type, timestamp, model, max_tokens, system (prompt metadata), messages (conversation history with role_counts, token counts, message_bytes), total_request_bytes. (RESPONSE) type, timestamp, duration_ms, first_byte_ms, status_code, response_bytes, usage (input_tokens, cache_creation_input_tokens, cache_read_input_tokens, output_tokens). IMPLICATION FOR PROJECTOR: Dual-stream format means each 'turn' in the projector's sense is actually 2 consecutive JSONL lines (request + response). Projector must: (1) group lines pairwise, (2) extract turn content from messages array in request, (3) note response usage statistics, (4) compress both into single TensorRecord per turn. Turn structure: messages array contains role (user/assistant), token counts, and text content (in messages_total_bytes). MESSAGE ARRAY STRUCTURE (inferred from line 1): role_counts dict (user: N, assistant: M), message_count, messages_total_bytes, tool_result_count, tool_result_bytes, tool_use_count, text_bytes, thinking_bytes. The actual message TEXT is not visible in sampled JSON (only sizes), but this is metadata about the turn sequence.

key_claims.text: JSONL log format is dual-stream: alternating request/response pairs (lines 1/2, 3/4, 5/6, ...), one JSON per line
Request line contains: type, timestamp, model, max_tokens, system (prompt), messages (metadata + text), total_request_bytes
Response line contains: type, timestamp, duration_ms, status_code, response_bytes, usage (input_tokens, cache stats, output_tokens)
One projector 'turn' = one request/response pair = two JSONL lines; grouping pairwise is required for parsing
Messages array includes role_counts (user/assistant), message_count, text_bytes, thinking_bytes; actual content text size is metadata (messages_total_bytes), not full text
Raw API logs contain system prompt (15K+ bytes), cached prompts, token usage statistics; minimal conversation content text visible in sampled lines

strands.title: Projector Input/Output: Turn-Level Compression Strategy
strands.content: CYCLE 40 ARCHITECTURE UPDATE: Projector takes (prior_tensor_d: TensorRecord, turn_request_json, turn_response_json) and produces tensor_d' (updated TensorRecord). Turn is now clearly defined as request/response pair from dual-stream format. CHALLENGE: actual conversation content (user message, assistant response TEXT) is not fully visible in JSONL peek — only metadata (token counts, byte counts, role counts). STRATEGY: (1) Accept that first pilot will work with PARTIAL content (extract what's visible: turn sequence, token counts, cache status, role markers), (2) Proceed with rule-based claim extraction on role_counts and metadata structure (infer claims from token patterns and turn dynamics), (3) Later cycles retrieve full text if needed (may require different file or session directory). MINIMAL VIABLE PROJECTOR (H0 cycle 40): Build parser that: (a) groups JSONL lines pairwise into turns, (b) extracts metadata (timestamps, tokens, roles, model), (c) initializes strands based on turn sequence (conversation flow, caching patterns, token dynamics), (d) assigns epistemic values based on usage patterns (assistant output vs user input), (e) tracks declared losses (any truncated/cached content), (f) emits valid TensorRecord. This trades full-content visibility for fast MVP implementation.

key_claims.text: Projector input: (prior_tensor_d, request_json, response_json) where request/response are parsed from dual-stream JSONL
Conversation TEXT content not directly visible in sampled JSONL lines; only metadata (token counts, byte counts, role counts) available
Minimal viable projector (H0 cycle 40) operates on metadata + structural patterns; claim extraction from role/token dynamics not from full text
Rule-based claim heuristics (H0): user_messages > threshold → user claim, assistant_tokens > threshold → assistant claim, cache_read > 0 → efficiency gain claim
Declared losses track: truncated content (if max_tokens hit), cache evictions, dropped turns due to token limits

strands.title: Cycle 40 Action Items: Build Minimal Viable Projector
strands.content: IMMEDIATE NEXT STEPS (cycle 40): (1) Get full line count: run wc -l on baseline_run1/logs/proxy_*.jsonl to determine total turn count (divide by 2 for request/response pairs). Expected: 31-62 turns if file is 62K tokens. (2) Read config.json and test_results.txt for baseline metrics and run metadata. (3) Design pairwise JSONL parser: read lines 0-1, 2-3, 4-5, etc. as turns; validate JSON, group by timestamp, extract (type, timestamp, model, role_counts, token_usage). (4) Design rule-based claim extractor: heuristics based on role_counts, token_bytes, cache_read, output_tokens. Example: if output_tokens > 100 → emit claim 'assistant produced substantive response'; if user + assistant alternate cleanly → emit claim 'conversation coherent'. (5) Initialize strands: detect patterns in token dynamics (conversation pacing), caching behavior (efficiency), or pre-define 3-5 strands (Conversation, Caching, Reasoning, Validation). (6) Implement epistemic assignment: assistant claims → 0.8 truth (responsive to user), cache claims → 0.85 truth (deterministic), user claims → 0.5 truth (user-provided). (7) Build TensorRecord builder and test on first 3 turns (lines 0-5). Target: valid Pydantic output, at least 3 claims, 2+ strands, compression ratio < 0.3.

key_claims.text: CYCLE 40 is implementation sprint: design and build H0 projector skeleton on real data (first 5 JSONL lines already peeked)
Parser strategy: pairwise grouping (lines 0-1, 2-3, ...) yields one turn per pair; validate JSON, extract metadata, construct turn dict
Claim heuristics (H0): role_counts reveal conversation structure; token counts reveal effort/length; cache_read reveals efficiency; output_tokens reveal response magnitude
Strand initialization: if turn_count < 10, pre-define 3 strands (Conversation, Caching, Reasoning); if >= 10, discover from token pattern clustering

declared_losses.description: JSONL peek (lines 1-5) revealed only metadata (token counts, role_counts, byte sizes); actual conversation text (user messages, assistant responses) not present in sampled lines. IMPLICATION: projector H0 will operate on structural patterns (token dynamics, turn sequence, role markers) rather than semantic content extraction. MITIGATION: Accept metadata-based claims as baseline; later cycles can retrieve full text from session/ directory or reconstruct from response_bytes if needed.

Could not read entire JSONL file to discover rare/edge-case fields or data quality issues (malformed JSON, truncated lines). Used offset-limit strategy to peek at first 5 lines only. IMPLICATION: schema inferred from sample may miss fields present only in later turns. MITIGATION: Implement robust JSON parser with error handling; monitor for exceptions during full-file run.

The 'messages' array in request JSON contains 'message_count' and 'messages_total_bytes' (total content size) but NOT the actual message text strings. JSONL lines sampled show metadata structure, not content. IMPLICATION: Cannot extract user/assistant message text directly from logs/proxy_*.jsonl. MITIGATION: Check if session/*.jsonl has different structure with actual text, or if response payload contains truncated content. For H0, work with role/token counts as proxies for claim extraction.

open_questions: CRITICAL: Are there other JSONL files that contain actual conversation text? Check session/*.jsonl (e.g., session/d5da3c7a-3c3c-4047-a4ee-276be57567c1.jsonl) to see if field structure differs and includes full message text.

LINE_COUNT: How many lines (turns) are in baseline_run1/logs/proxy_*.jsonl? Run: wc -l /home/tony/projects/pichay/experiments/baseline_run1/logs/proxy_20260302_024551.jsonl. Estimated: 6-124 lines (3-62 turns). Needed to scope H0 implementation.
CONFIG_METADATA: What settings are in /home/tony/projects/pichay/experiments/baseline_run1/config.json? (model, temperature, max_tokens, other hyperparameters?) Needed to understand run context.
TEST_METRICS: What baseline scores/metrics are in test_results.txt? Are they used to validate H0 projector output later?
SESSION_VS_LOGS: Is session/*.jsonl the processed/cleaned version of logs/proxy_*.jsonl, or a parallel record of the same turns? Which is source-of-truth for projector input?
RESPONSE_TEXT: Does response JSON payload (response_bytes field, lines 2/4/6) contain truncated assistant text? If so, how to extract full text from line 2/4/6?
FILE_STRUCTURE_DISCOVERY: Cycle 40 action: Read first 1-2 lines of session/*.jsonl to compare field structure vs logs/. Report: identical schema or specialized fields?
PYDANTIC_SCHEMA: Is there an existing ApachetaBaseModel or TensorRecord definition in the codebase? Need to read schema to ensure projector output is compatible.
H0_STRAND_COUNT: Pre-define 3-5 strands for H0, or discover from first 10 turns? Recommend: pre-defined (Conversation Flow, Token Efficiency, Cache Behavior) for simplicity.

CLAIM_EXTRACTION_FROM_METADATA: Specific heuristics needed. Proposal: (1) if output_tokens > 500 → 'long_response' claim, (2) if cache_read > 0 → 'cache_hit' claim, (3) if role_counts.user == role_counts.assistant → 'balanced_dialogue' claim. Validate against manual annotation if time permits.
instructions_for_next:
[
  "CYCLE 40 (IMMEDIATE): Get line count. Run: wc -l /home/tony/projects/pichay/experiments/baseline_run1/logs/proxy_20260302_024551.jsonl. Report exact number of lines. Divide by 2 to get turn count (since each turn = request + response pair).",
  "CYCLE 40 (IMMEDIATE): Read config.json. Open /home/tony/projects/pichay/experiments/baseline_run1/config.json (should be small), extract: model name, temperature, max_tokens, any projector-relevant settings. Document in projector design notes.",
  "CYCLE 40 (IMMEDIATE): Read test_results.txt. Open /home/tony/projects/pichay/experiments/baseline_run1/test_results.txt, extract: baseline metrics (accuracy, latency, token efficiency), run date/duration. Use as reference for H0 validation later.",
  "CYCLE 40 (IMMEDIATE): Compare session vs logs schema. Read first 1-2 lines of /home/tony/projects/pichay/experiments/baseline_run1/session/d5da3c7a-3c3c-4047-a4ee-276be57567c1.jsonl (use offset=0, limit=2 same as logs). Pretty-print first JSON, list all field names. Compare vs logs schema (line 1 of proxy_*.jsonl). Report: identical fields or new fields present in session?",
  "CYCLE 40: Synthesize JSONL schema document. Consolidate: (1) Field names from logs line 1, (2) Field names from session line 1, (3) Type of each field, (4) Projector relevance (PRIMARY: type, timestamp, role_counts, token usage; SECONDARY: model, max_tokens, metadata; IGNORE: internal request/response details). Create schema_discovery.md.",
  "CYCLE 40: Design pairwise JSONL parser. Pseudocode: (1) Open file, read all lines, (2) for i in range(0, len(lines), 2): request_line = lines[i], response_line = lines[i+1], (3) parse JSON for each, (4) validate type (request should be 'request', response should be 'response_stream'), (5) extract metadata dict per turn, (6) yield turn dict with {request_data, response_data, turn_index}. Implement in Python (src/pichay/projector/parser.py).",
  "CYCLE 40: Design metadata-based claim extractor. Rule heuristics: (a) if output_tokens >= 1000 → claim: 'assistant_verbose_response' (truth: 0.85), (b) if output_tokens < 100 → claim: 'assistant_brief_response' (truth: 0.80), (c) if cache_read > 0 → claim: 'cache_efficiency_gain' (truth: 0.90), (d) if input_tokens > 10000 → claim: 'large_context_window' (truth: 0.92), (e) if role_counts.user == role_counts.assistant → claim: 'balanced_conversation' (truth: 0.88). Start with 3-5 heuristics; iterate post-validation.",
  "CYCLE 40: Design strand initialization. Given turn_count (from line count), choose strategy: (a) if turn_count < 10: pre-define 3 strands: 'Conversation' (user/assistant dialogue), 'Efficiency' (token/cache metrics), 'Reasoning' (longer outputs). (b) if turn_count >= 10: sample turns 1, (turn_count//2), turn_count, extract token patterns, use as clusters for strand names. Recommend (a) for H0 simplicity.",
  "CYCLE 40: Design epistemic assignment heuristics. Proposal: (1) assistant claims (identified by turn role) → default 0.80 truth, (2) cache claims → 0.90 truth (deterministic), (3) token/efficiency claims → 0.85 truth (measurable), (4) adjust by confidence: if output_tokens > median → +0.05 truth, if cache_read > 0 → +0.05 truth, cap at 1.0. Indeterminacy: 0.10-0.15 (model outputs are inherently uncertain). Falsity: 0.05-0.10 (rare, only for contradictory claims).",
  "CYCLE 40: Build projector skeleton. Modules: (1) parser.py (pairwise JSONL reader), (2) extractor.py (claim heuristics), (3) strand_assigner.py (map claims to strands), (4) epistemic_assigner.py (T/I/F values), (5) tensor_builder.py (Pydantic TensorRecord instantiation). Test each module independently on first 1-2 turns. Target: zero import errors, valid JSON parsing, non-empty claim/strand output.",
  "CYCLE 40: Implement parser module. Input: file path. Output: iterator of turn dicts (one per request/response pair). Must handle: (a) line pairing (validate odd/even structure), (b) JSON parsing (catch errors, log malformed lines), (c) timestamp extraction and sorting. Test: read proxy_*.jsonl, iterate first 3 turns, print turn metadata.",
  "CYCLE 40: Implement claim extractor. Input: turn dict (request_data + response_data). Output: list of claim dicts (text, source, confidence, epistemic). Apply heuristics (if output_tokens > 1000 → claim; if cache_read > 0 → claim; etc). Test: apply to first 3 turns, verify at least 2 claims per turn (if heuristics sound).",
  "CYCLE 40: Implement strand assigner. Input: list of claims. Output: dict {strand_name → [claims]}. Use pre-defined strands (Conversation, Efficiency, Reasoning) and simple keyword matching: 'cache' → Efficiency, 'output_tokens' → Reasoning, 'user/assistant' → Conversation. If claim matches no strand → assign to 'Other'. Test: assign claims from first 3 turns, verify all claims mapped.",
  "CYCLE 40: Implement epistemic assigner. Input: claim dict. Output: updated claim with {truth, indeterminacy, falsity} ∈ [0, 1]. Apply rules: default 0.80/0.10/0.10, adjust by heuristics. Ensure sum ≈ 1.0 (normalize if needed). Test: verify sum property across 20+ sampled claims.",
  "CYCLE 40: Implement TensorRecord builder. Input: strands dict, claims list, loss list, open questions, instructions. Output: TensorRecord Pydantic instance (frozen, extra='forbid'). Validate all required fields present, no null values. Test: construct from first-turn data, verify schema compliance (no exceptions).",
  "CYCLE 40: Build integration test (mini pilot). Load first 3 turns from proxy_*.jsonl via parser. For turn 0: init empty tensor_d, call projector(tensor_d, turn_0) → tensor_d_0 (TensorRecord). For turn 1: call projector(tensor_d_0, turn_1) → tensor_d_1. For turn 2: call projector(tensor_d_1, turn_2) → tensor_d_2. Log output. Check: (1) valid Pydantic? (2) ≥3 strands total? (3) ≥5 claims total? (4) declared_losses non-empty? (5) T/I/F sums valid? If all PASS → green flag for full-file run.",
  "CYCLE 40: Measure compression on 3-turn pilot. Compute: (a) original_bytes = sum of turn 0/1/2 total_request_bytes + response_bytes, (b) projected_bytes = sum of tensor_0/1/2 JSON sizes. Ratio = projected / original. Target: 0.15-0.30. If > 0.4, need to drop more content (compress harder). If < 0.1, may be losing too much (check claims/strands are populated).",
  "CYCLE 40: Diagnostics report on 3-turn pilot. Output: (1) strands count + names, (2) claims count + top 5 claims by type, (3) declared_losses count + categories, (4) open_questions count, (5) compression ratio, (6) any warnings/errors. Summarize: does projector behave as intended (semantic compression + loss tracking)?",
  "CYCLE 40 ACCEPTANCE CHECKPOINT: 3-turn pilot must PASS: (1) No crashes, valid Pydantic output (X3 turns), (2) ≥3 unique strands identified, (3) ≥5 distinct claims extracted, (4) compression ratio ∈ [0.1, 0.5], (5) declared_losses.length > 0 (losses tracked). If PASS → proceed to full-file run (cycle 40-41). If FAIL: debug specific module (parser? extractor? builder?) and re-run.",
  "CYCLE 40-41: Scale to full file. Once 3-turn pilot PASSES, run projector on ALL turns in proxy_*.jsonl. Use parser to iterate all turn pairs. For each turn N: call projector(tensor_{N-1}, turn_N) → tensor_N. Emit each tensor_N to output log (JSONL or Python pickle). Collect aggregate metrics: total_claims, total_strands, total_losses, final_compression_ratio.",
  "CYCLE 40-41: Full-file metrics. After processing all turns: (1) Plot compression_ratio over turns (should stabilize), (2) Plot claims_per_turn (should have mean > 2), (3) Plot losses_per_turn (should trend down if losses are consolidated), (4) Report final strand count (expected: 5-10 for a 30-60 turn conversation), (5) Report loss categories (e.g., truncated: 15%, merged: 8%, other: 7%).",
  "CYCLE 41: ACCEPTANCE CHECKPOINT v2. Full-file run must PASS: (1) Pydantic validation on 100% of turns (zero exceptions), (2) Compression ratio 0.15-0.35 across full file (lossy but stable), (3) Final strand count ∈ [3, 12] (reasonable topical diversity), (4) Total claims = clusters of 3-7 per major topic (claims are grouped), (5) Declared losses account for <40% of original content (acceptably lossy). If PASS → freeze rule-based projector, document schema/heuristics, proceed to Phase 1.5 (apply to all 15 runs). If FAIL: iterate heuristics (cycle 41) or debug schema (cycle 41).",
  "DOCUMENTATION (cycle 40-41): Create cycle_40_artifacts/ folder: (1) schema_discovery.md (field names, types, from logs + session JSONL), (2) design_heuristics.md (claim extraction rules, strand logic, epistemic rules — all deterministic), (3) projector_code.py (full implementation, well-commented), (4) 3turn_pilot_report.txt (output from 3-turn test), (5) full_file_metrics.csv (per-turn metrics from full run). Save in /home/tony/.claude/projects/pichay/memory/ for next cycle.",
  "RESOURCE CHECK (cycle 40): Time budget is finite. If 3-turn pilot completes by mid-cycle 40, full-file run is feasible end-of-cycle. If skeleton/parser blocks, prioritize parser implementation (most critical). If full-file takes > 50% of cycle time, defer detailed validation to cycle 41. Main goal cycle 40: working MVP, not perfect accuracy.",
  "CYCLE 41-42 (CONTINGENT): If full-file pilot PASSES CHECKPOINT v2, run on all 15 baseline runs. Aggregate metrics across runs: (1) mean compression ratio + std dev, (2) mean strand count + std dev, (3) loss distribution (are patterns consistent across runs?). Check hypothesis: projector is stable (low variance) across different conversations. If PASS: H0 is validated, ready for Phase 2 (LLM-assisted refinement or learning-based strand discovery)."
]
